task_completion.description: A tiddler titled 'Interactive Deployment Generator' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title field of the tiddler in edit mode, or the tiddler title in view mode, reads exactly 'Interactive Deployment Generator'.

task_completion.description: The tiddler is tagged with 'DevOps' and 'Runbook'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tag pills for 'DevOps' and 'Runbook' are visible just below the tiddler title.

task_completion.description: Input fields for application name, image tag, and port number are present and bound to the correct state tiddlers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In edit mode, look for TiddlyWiki widget code like `<$edit-text tiddler='$:/state/deploy/app'/>` (and similarly for tag and port). In view mode, look for three text input boxes.

task_completion.description: A code block for the Docker command is present and uses the input values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In edit mode, look for the Docker command text containing transclusions, e.g., `docker run -d --name {{$:/state/deploy/app}} -p {{$:/state/deploy/port}}:80 myregistry.internal/{{$:/state/deploy/app}}:{{$:/state/deploy/tag}}`. In view mode, look for a code block showing the rendered Docker command.

task_completion.description: A code block for the Kubernetes command is present and uses the input values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In edit mode, look for the Kubernetes command text containing transclusions, e.g., `kubectl create deployment {{$:/state/deploy/app}} --image=myregistry.internal/{{$:/state/deploy/app}}:{{$:/state/deploy/tag}} --port={{$:/state/deploy/port}}`. In view mode, look for a code block showing the rendered Kubernetes command.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the TiddlyWiki GUI to create the tiddler.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the TiddlyWiki interface, clicking the '+' button, or editing within the TiddlyWiki text editor, rather than using a terminal or external script.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI by directly editing TiddlyWiki backend files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser window with TiddlyWiki is visible and actively used throughout the trajectory.

integrity.description: The agent used genuine TiddlyWiki syntax for inputs and transclusions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The source code shows `<$edit-text>` and `{{...}}` rather than hardcoded static text that just looks like the final output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The tiddler must be titled 'Interactive Deployment Generator' and tagged with 'DevOps' and 'Runbook'. It must use the state tiddlers '$:/state/deploy/app', '$:/state/deploy/tag', and '$:/state/deploy/port' for the inputs and command templates.
